Patch Set 3:

Does having cpt be  a negative number mean anything?

Patch Set 3:

Actually No for now. But I'd like to make cpu id usage in our code similar to kernel usage for cpu identification. I don't like to use different types for the same items.
Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 3:

Dmitry can you update the commit message to point out that you want to bring the lustre CPT code into alignment with the linux kernel.

parents.subject: LU-7819 tests: fix checkfiemap for stripecount > 1

commit.subject: LU-8703 libcfs: use int type for CPT identification.
commit.message:
LU-8703 libcfs: use int type for CPT identification.

This patch bring the lustre CPT code into alignment with the Linux kernel.

 * use int type for CPT identification like CPU identification.
 * fix coding style.

Change-Id: I5df33f37757eb6d47ab4e824ec1b800a40730f61
Signed-off-by: Dmitry Eremin <dmitry.eremin@intel.com>
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/23304
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@yahoo.com>
Reviewed-by: Doug Oucharek <doug.s.oucharek@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
